entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content:
[Just upgraded to CR 2020.]

When loading a report that uses data from a previously exported Excel ODBC in CR2016, I get the error: "Failed to load database information. Details: Access/Excel(DAO) connection is no longer supported, use another alternative if possible, e.g. ODBC/JDBC." Therefore, I think exporting to ODBC Excel/Access is no longer a viable solution in CR 2020. I can't even access the Excel ODBC data [using the same method] from the CR2016 export file.

This is unfortunate because I need to access the same Oracle database with two different logins for data for one report. CR [or Oracle setup] doesn't seem to allow this. I have tried a number of different methods to accomplish it without success. So in CR2016, I had to retrieve data into Report1, move the previous data in the "excel odbc file" by clearing the excel Name Manager info, change the excel tab name, then re-export the new CR data to the same Excel ODBC file, then use it as a secondary data source for Report2 without needing to modify this Report2. [It was a little clunky, but got the job done.]

For the new CR2020 install, I have installed the MSOffice 365 Database Engine [and 2016] and various items. The "Export" to ODBC Excel/Access does not seem to work, regardless of AccessEngine install. Attempting to get it to work in another way, I have successfully connected to the ODBC file using the MS ODBC configuration settings [start menu ODBC, each file needs a new connection in Drive C:, then create a New Connection using ODBC (RDO) in CR2020]. I currently can only retrieve data from the ODBC file. I am not sure how to update data in the ODBC file using this setup. I may need to incorporate SQL Update commands in Report1 [if possible] to update the external Excel ODBC file. Then use that ODBC connection as secondary input data in Report2.

Another possiblity would be to just use the CR2020 excel output file as a datasource without the ODBC connection. I have not tried this before, but will attempt this in the near future. Then, every time the excel is output from Report1 and overwritten, the data is updated for Report2.

Any other work arounds or ideas are appreciated.

entry.title: What is the alternative to app: Maintain Sample-drawing procedure in SAP public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi,

At the moment, the Maintain Sample-drawing procedure app is not available in SAP Public Cloud. What is the alternative to make de calculation of sample-drawing?  

Thank you. 

Ana

entry.title: Re: How to find old values in set ids?
entry.content: HI Lakshmi, Did you found the way to get the old values? We are also facing same issue. due to movement of Validation all the set values got overwritten. Would be helpful if you can share the solution you have implemented. Regards

entry.title: Re: Consumption of an API within a Custom POD Plugin in S...
entry.content: Hi Tobias, sorry can I ask please where can I find the code for the function getPublicApiRestDataSourceUri?. I can see this function being called in the sap examples but cannot see its logic? : https://github.com/search?q=repo%3ASAP-samples%2Fdigital-manufacturing-extension-samples%20getPublicApiRestDataSourceUri()&type=code

entry.title: Calculation Methods in Depreciation Key
entry.content:
Hello All, 

I'm trying to better understand how the SAP system prioritizes multiple calculation methods in the depreciation key. I am familiar with the different calculation methods (Base Method, Declining-Balance, Maximum Amount, and Multi-Level Methods), as well as the basics of Straight-Line Depreciation and Written-Down Value.

My question is: when we assign multiple calculation methods in the depreciation key and each method results in a different depreciation value, how does SAP determine which method takes priority for the asset depreciation?

Thanks, Sateesh Kankanampati

entry.title: Calculation Methods in Depreciation Key
entry.content:
Hello All, 

I'm trying to better understand how the SAP system prioritizes multiple calculation methods in the depreciation key. I am familiar with the different calculation methods (Base Method, Declining-Balance, Maximum Amount, and Multi-Level Methods), as well as the basics of Straight-Line Depreciation and Written-Down Value.

My question is: when we assign multiple calculation methods in the depreciation key and each method results in a different depreciation value, how does SAP determine which method takes priority for the asset depreciation?

entry.title: Re: How to query Users ad their own Authorization Groups
entry.content:
In SAP B1 you can associate one or more Group to a user. Then you can assign to the Group some authorizations.

All the users having that group inherit the same authorizations.

If you need to know which users belong to a specific group you can use the query above.

Regards

DLE

entry.title: Confirmation Text
entry.content:
Hi, 

Is there a way to export confirmation short text and long text out of SAP? 

Thank you

entry.title: SAC Advanced Formula - Conditional Check for Existence of Records Across a Scope in FOREACH Loop
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working with an advanced formula in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Planning to selectively copy data records based on a condition. The goal is to check if an employee has any record with Datasource = "INPUT" and, if so, to copy that employee's records from Datasource = "BW" to new records under Datasource = "BW_Override". For employees without any INPUT records, no action should be taken.

Here is a simplified structure of my data:

Start MonthEnd MonthDateCost CentreDatasourceGL AccountEmployeeHR AccountFlagFTE
2024072029060000008216BW#7048##1
2024072029060000008216INPUT#7048FORTNIGHTLY_SALARY#0.5
..............................

To achieve this, I'm trying to use a FOREACH loop with an IF condition. My current approach is as follows:

CONFIG.TIME_HIERARCHY = CALENDARYEAR
CONFIG.FLIPPING_SIGN_ACCORDING_ACCTYPE = OFF
CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF

// Define the scope for relevant Datasource values
MEMBERSET [d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = ("INPUT", "BW", "BW_Override")

FOREACH.BOOKED [d/PLN_EMPLOYEE]
    // Broad-scope check if there's any record with Datasource = INPUT for the current employee
    IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "INPUT") > 0 THEN
        // Copy records from BW to BW_Override for employees with INPUT records
        DATA([d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "BW_Override", [d/PLN_FLAG] = "X") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "BW")
    ENDIF
ENDFOR

However, it seems the IF condition is not functioning as expected. I suspect RESULTLOOKUP in the IF statement may be performing a set-wise check rather than aggregating across all records for each employee in the loop, so it fails to correctly detect the presence of Datasource = "INPUT".

My questions:

  1. Is there a way in SAC advanced formulas to check for the existence of records within a scope (e.g., if any record exists with Datasource = "INPUT" for an employee)?
  2. How can I ensure the IF condition only triggers when at least one Datasource = "INPUT" record is found for the employee, rather than checking on a set-wise basis?

Any guidance on structuring this condition or alternatives for achieving the desired result would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

SAP Analytics Cloud for planning SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: Fiscal year variants in company code FIL have differing start dates
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA for product Compliance NEW Dangerous Goods Ma...

entry.title: Fill in a survey on behalf
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I would like to know if is it possible to fill in a survey on behalf of a user in SAP LMS.

In the case the survey was done in a paper and we would like to report it in SAP LMS for example.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Trigger an outbound Idoc when a PR is created.
entry.content: There are samples in online documentation

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Product compliance - Safety Data Sheet creat...
entry.content: Many thanks for the responses. Would the 3E Hybrid adapter solution be regarded as interim, considering that EHS classic will be phased out in future releases? from a strategic point of view, I don't think it makes sense to implement from scratch a hybrid classic/new PC solution if classic will eventually be phased out. Especially since in the roadmap, there will be plans to have safety data sheet creation and maintenance in new PC. I guess this solution is more for customers who already have a EHS classic solution and want to start using the new PC solution. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Fiscal year variants in company code FIL have differing start dates
entry.content: Hi.. I have configured as per #note 2220152. But how to view Assets by using two Fiscal year variants in Asset Register? It is only reflecting one fiscal year (Same fiscal year for both Leading ledger and Representative ledger) for all depreciation areas. What if I want to view both Jan to Dec along with Apr to Mar.. How to generate that report and calculation using two different fiscal year variants?

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content: hi

entry.title: SAP CAP Authentication Error
entry.content:
Hi all,

A beginner to SAP CAP here. I've created a CAP application with Catalog Service, SAPUI5 app in app directory and binding it to Catalog Service. 

Normally the application was running locally before adding xsuaa. However, after adding it, it stopped working and started giving 401 error. Since xsuaa is on the btp side, I thought it would work after deploying to the btp side, but I continued to get 401 error in the same way

Here's my some code files:
mta.yaml:

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: mycapapp
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: mycapapp-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    requires:
      - name: mycapapp-db
      - name: mycapapp-auth
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
      readiness-health-check-type: http
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm
  - name: mycapapp-db-deployer
    type: hdb
    path: gen/db
    requires:
      - name: mycapapp-db
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
  - name: mycapapp-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: mycapapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - capapp.zip
          name: capapp
          target-path: resources/
  - name: capapp
    type: html5
    path: app/capapp
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms:
        []
resources:
  - name: mycapapp-db
    type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
    parameters:
      service: hana
      service-plan: hdi-shared
  - name: mycapapp-auth
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        tenant-mode: dedicated
        xsappname: capapp
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application
  - name: mycapapp-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: mycapapp-html5-srv
      service-plan: app-host
  - name: mycapapp-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: mycapapp-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

package.json:

{
  "name": "mycapapp",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A simple CAP project.",
  "repository": "<Add your repository here>",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "@sap/cds": "^8",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4",
    "express": "^4",
    "passport": "^0.7.0",
    "@cap-js/hana": "^1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-types": "^0.6",
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^1",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^8",
    "@sap/ux-specification": "UI5-1.130"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cds-serve",
    "debug": "cds watch --profile hybrid",
    "watch-capapp": "cds watch --open capapp/webapp/index.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false"
  },
  "cds": {
    "fiori": {
      "preview": true
    },
    "server": {
      "index": true
    },
    "requires": {
      "[hybrid]": {
        "db": "hana",
        "auth": "xsuaa"
      }
    },
    "sql": {
      "native_hana_associations": false
    }
  }
}

xs-security.json:

{
  "xsappname": "capapp",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.Admin",
      "description": "Admin"
    },
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.View",
      "description": "View"
    }
  ],
  "attributes": [],
  "role-templates": [
    {
      "name": "Admin",
      "description": "generated",
      "scope-references": [
        "$XSAPPNAME.Admin"
      ],
      "attribute-references": []
    },
    {
      "name": "View",
      "description": "generated",
      "scope-references": [
        "$XSAPPNAME.View"
      ],
      "attribute-references": []
    }
  ],
  "oauth2-configuration": {
    "credential-types": [
      "binding-secret",
      "x509"
    ],
    "redirect-uris": [
      "http://*.localhost:4004/**",
      "https://*.hana.ondemand.com/**",
      "https://*.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/**"
    ]
  }
}

xs-app.json:

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/$1",
      "destination": "srv-api",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": true
    },
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

My file structure:

entry.title: Re: How can I make ALV fill the entire screen?
entry.content: First enlarge the size of the screen. Therefore go to Attributes of the screen. There you will find Lines/Columns within frame "Other Attributes".

entry.title: Re: FI substitution not triggered from SD Invoice
entry.content: Don't use callup point 0003

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP UI5 Application
entry.content: Hi @nicoschoenteich, Thanks. I rearranged the routes in xs-app.json file as you suggested. which works for me.

entry.title: Extract complained use for specific email campaign category
entry.content:
Is there any way to extract the complaint user for specific email category / groups of email campaigns?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Extract complained use for specific email campaign ca...
entry.content:
This is not possible to be checked using the reports at Emarsys UI, but you can use open data for that by joining both email_campaigns_v2 and email_complaints views based on the campaign_id field. You can read more details about each of those views at the documents below.

  • SAP Emarsys Help: Email campaigns v2 view
  • SAP Emarsys Help: Email complaints

entry.title: Re: FI substitution not triggered from SD Invoice
entry.content: If required look at customer exits of Enhancemebnts SDVFX001 (header) or SDVFX002 (customer item)

Get an overview of Sustainability at SAP, featuring an in-depth look at Sustainable Procurement and also a live demo with sustainable procurement use cases.

⏰ Time: 13:30 – 14:30 (GMT+2) (6 - 7 PM IST)

Registration: https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/4694a633-7dca-48a8-ae9e-e614cddfe5de@42f7676c-f455-423c-82f6-dc2d99791af7

entry.title: Re: Maintain Bill of Material Fiori App not working
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: Consumption of an API within a Custom POD Plugin in S...
entry.content: getPublicApiRestDataSourceUri is a function from the plugin framework. I am not sure if it is possible to look at the source code, but it is referenced in the documentation. Either directly here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/digital-manufacturing-extension-samples/blob/main/documentation/jsdocs/sap_dm_dme_podfoundation_controller_PluginViewController.html or you could download the JS Docs from GitHub and check it locally

entry.title: Importing Electronic Bank Statements automatically in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
How to automatically import electronic bank statements to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environment?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Importing Electronic Bank Statements automatically in...
entry.content:
You can have the system automatically import your bank statements directly from your bank's system by having purchased and activated SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity or you can use the Bank Statement - Post API.  Please refer to the below documentations for more details:

How do Bank statements get into your system?

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

3516767 - Bank Integration for Incoming receipts (2YM) - Payment Message Handling

APIs for Receivables Management

entry.title: Re: Enhancing Project Outcomes with SAP: Best Practices from a Project Manager's Perspective
entry.content:
Absolutely, I can imagine how balancing those timelines would be quite the juggling act! Kudos to you for successfully navigating that complexity. It sounds like you’ve mastered the art of keeping everyone on the same page, even when the IT team’s in sprint mode and finance or operations needs to catch their breath to meet their specific requirements.

Most of my experience is in warehouse management, shipping, and logistics, so I haven’t had the chance to be on an implementation team, which seems like a fun challenge. My background is primarily in managing personalities, meeting production deadlines, handling inventory, and positioning team members based on their strengths and weaknesses.

entry.title: Re: CLM - Lease In contract according to IFRS16 rules: fu...
entry.content:
Hi Cedric,
thank you again for your question.
My previous answer contained the overall capabilities you have to either influence the valuation and interest capabilities.
But as stated in the note 2662137 (point 15), the frequency term of the valuation rule just considers 2 settings: Frequency and Calculation method
So the prorated setting is not considered as it makes no sense to calculate the interest payments non-prorated (for the whole month) as it is based on the net present value. Otherwise you would post more interests than intended.
Kind regards
Nils

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content:
That number you gave is not a support Incident but a sales number, not the same thing.

Actually you can't buy a support contract for Crystal Reports, no longer available for CR Designer unless you purchase CRS, BOE or some other Crystal Product.

R&D confirmed it's and issue and will be providing a fix in some patch, possibly the next one for CR 2020 and other CRS/BOE products.

Thanks for reporting the issue...

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content:
When you need to use 2 different log ons you must use a subreport, it can have a separate DB logon UI. You can't use the main report with 2 DB's unless you can link them somehow.

Try this, Open CR Designer and click on File - Log on or off server and connect to 1 and then again to the other DB Server.

Then open the report and see if you can set Location for each connection using Set Location...

Other than that your only option is to use a subreport for the second user and link the Main report to the Subreport.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Fixed Asset Legacy Data Migration
entry.content: Nice Information.

entry.title: Changing the label of a section in a CAP application using SAP Fiori Elements and UI5
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on a CAP application and we want to change the text from the label of a section inside an object page to contain both the initial label and after it to have a number between "()". This number is saved in the proprieties of the entity whose object page we are on. We have seen that there is a way to add the count like that using the table property "showCounts:true", but we are looking to add a different value.

Is there any way to do this? We want it to look like this:

 

 

 

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content: Don, that's the "DigReqNo" in the original first response I received from SAP, not to mention it's referred to as the "Ticket Number" in the email subject, so I just assumed that was my case number. Sorry about that. Otherwise, WHAT was confirmed to be an issue? I realize you don't owe me more details, but right now you're like a godfather benevolently helping me out and I can't express enough gratitude for your help!

entry.title: Re: /LRN/S4D400_APT table doesn't exist in ABAP Cloud Course
entry.content: But it is not free? Almost U$ 1400 ?

entry.title: Re: Unable to specify the RFC system in migration cockpit...
entry.content: Thank you Heike. I have been using the MC for a while now. But the challenge with not being able to edit the source system has been an issue, that I even raised with SAP. This is particularly applicable when you have an existing project and you add more objects to it.

entry.title: Re: How to Use Custom Fields for Pricing in Sales Documents
entry.content:
Hello @WesleyZhang,

In our case we want to update prices automatically without pushing update price button. Is there a way to do that?

entry.title: Re: CR2020 viewer print output is incorrect when PrintMod...
entry.content:
There is a difference... let me explain

Crystal Reports is built using Visual Studio C++ and it uses direct access to the DEVMODE structure, that's where Microsoft saves the Printer info.

So in Crystal Designer it directly accesses the DEVMODE structure when printing.

This is the same info SAVED in the RPT file. In Visual Studio using the Edit Report feature it use an Embedded Report Designer called CRAXDDRT.

It's a COM dll that wraps around crpe32.dll, that's the core report engine which uses the DEVMODE structure for printing.

So now the reason you see 2 different Print UI's is because P2P uses the saved info in the RPT file and doesn't use the .NET Printer Dialog.

I forget the actual names of the Printer Dialog dll's...

So when using P2P it uses the crpe32.dll to access the printer dialog.

When using a .NET App the POC API uses the .NET Printer Dialog box which then wraps around crpe32.dll and things are not 100%. Which is why the default margin in POC is .51 inches, note even if you set those values the .NET values is ignored, internally that is the smallest margin POC is capable of using.

The other thing you have to into account is the Custom Paper Size you are using, .NET will attempt to "match" the closest paper size available. It actually queries the Printer to see what paper size is closest to what is set in the Report.

To sum up:

CR Designer accesses DEVMODE structure directly.

CR Basic, which is what the Embedded Report Designer is in Visual Studio uses what is saved in the RPT file.

.NET POC API uses MS's .NET Printer Dialog box which is a .NET wrapper around the DEVMODE structure and is not always 100% compatible.

Also be aware those PDF print drivers are also not 100% perfect, when I worked at SAP I escalated a few issue with CurePDF and MS PDF printer, in all cases it was a limitation of those print drivers.

I suggest contacting CutePDF and explain to them you are seeing the top Margin being increased and see if they can fix their driver.

So again, bottom line is there is nothing CR can do to fix this.

FYI - I used my test app and rather than printing from the CR Viewers Print button I set the printer to my Lexmark printer, change it to landscape, best I could do and selected Legal paper. I did not see the header space as you did.

FYII - I did the same as you and the CR Viewers POC did the same as your test.

Work around, use your own print Button rather than the one built into CR Viewers. There was a test app that turned off CR Viewers print button but I can't find the KBA, it wasn't supported because the Button ID's can change any time..

entry.title: Re: Multiple bank in bank selection (FPY1 Payment run) depending the contract account
entry.content:
Hi Farid, 

There is a concept Bank selection control where in you can optimize the Bank selection.

IMG-->Financial Accounting-->Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable-->Payments-->Incoming/Outgoing Payment Creation-->Define Specifications for Paying Company Code

Select the payment method and then on clicking display you will see the settings.

In your scenario, you need to select optimization by Bank group option and then update all the bank master data with the same bank group for which you want to generate the same file.

And then on executing FPY1, it will always attempt to select two banks that belong to the same group.

Hope it helps.

Thanks

Amlan

entry.title: Re: SAP Payroll HCM Advanced Leave Payment (Annual Leave)...
entry.content: Mashup is mainly for Infotypes and not transactions. Once employee is locked, no payroll will be trigerred.

entry.title: Re: Goods issue to sampling from QI in /SCWM/ADGI posting...
entry.content:
Hi Pranab,

SMPL GI process from Q* stock type works like from F* stock type: for both Q* and F*, the system post a 333 movement type (in MM).

Best Regards

Roberto

entry.title: How to display simple text on an embedded Word document within SAP GUI using OLE without opening an
entry.content:
I'm working on an SAP ABAP program that embeds a Microsoft Word document into the SAP GUI container using i_oi_document_proxy from SAP's Office Integration. I need to display a simple text, retrieved from an internal table, directly inside this embedded document without opening an external Word instance. My goal is to ensure the text appears within the integrated Word document in the SAP GUI container itself.

i wanted to work with ole2 at first as it offers simple manipulation for the document but it opens a new instance of word independent and not in the container , i didn t know how to make ole2 work on the document created with i_oi_document_proxy. is there a way to do so!!! if not if there a way to display a simple text from the abap code to the integrated word document!!!

I've tried several methods to load and display the text:

Populating the internal table with sample text and attempting to load it into the embedded document. Using OLE2, but this approach often creates a separate Word instance outside the SAP GUI container, which is not what I want. My expectation is to use OLE (or an alternative approach, if available) to manipulate the embedded Word document directly, displaying text in it without launching Word independently.

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Contracts Certification Contains lessons th...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Strategic Sourcing refers to a suite of products that address your company's holistic sourcing needs, including tendering (called SAP Ariba Sourcing) and contract management (refer to as SAP Ariba Contracts) activities.

entry.title: Automatic contract determination in PR created via MRP in S4
entry.content:
Hi to all, we are in a conversion project from ECC to S4.

In this case we have this scenario:

Material AAA.  There is a valid Qty contract 46000001

NO Info record. No Source list created. No SourceList relevant in Purchasing view in material.

When running MD03 the program creates a PR but with this contract assinged. WHY?? 

as I said, there is no IR and no SourceList.

I was not able to find a response to this.

Any ideas?

entry.title: Re: Consumption of an API within a Custom POD Plugin in S...
entry.content: Thanks for your quick response but I am still struggling to understand: do I need to call that function in my custom plugin as it is already available in the framework?. No parameters have to be passed?

entry.title: Adding specific image for each item
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

 

Do you know if is it possible to add specific image for each item in SAP LMS?

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Compatibility Scope Matrix for Production Planning.. 2025 is around the corner!!
entry.content:
The Readiness Check Report helps us understand the mandatory changes when moving from ECC to S/4HANA or just upgrading to the newest version on S/4HANA. Through this information, all the customers will be able to plan their projects accordingly to have the system ready for a smooth transition. From a functional perspective, this report shows us 2 main pieces of information, the first one is the Simplification Item list, which highlights the mandatory changes and the items that are out of the architecture target in S/4HANA and won't be considered for further innovations. The second one is the Compatibility Scope Matrix, which lists the compatibility packages (CP), this contains the details of the functionalities that have a limited use right to SAP S/4HANA and recommends the solution alternative and the strategy to move forward.

This blog will explain the common items relevant as part of the compatibility scope under the Production Planning module, describing the details and the alternative function to be considered when replacing it. Functions that are part of the compatibility scope can be used just until the expiry date of the compatibility pack license, meaning at the end of 2025. Even when SAP recently announced the maintenance extension for SAP Business Suite 7 until the end of 2027, the usage rights of the compatibility packs were not included in this extension.

Luckily and because the complexity of each item, there is a specific extension of compatibility scope usage rights for a few modules until 2030. Under manufacturing, this applies to selected items under the component of Production Planning in Process Industry (PP-PI). If you are interested in checking the full details list of the compatibility Scope Matrix please review the attachments of SAP Note 2269324. There you will find the components marked with (D), meaning whose are the items relevant for the extension.

My intention through this blog is to mainly aware and remind all the readers about what items are relevant considering the timing! But in the end, is up to each customer to decide when they will keep it or will start to migrate to the designated alternative functionality. The decision, of at which point in time this will be done, should be taken early in advance to plan accordingly for the migration efforts!! Keep in mind the end of 2025 is around the corner!!

Now, let’s get started and finally introduce some of the items for the Production Planning scope. Keep in mind this list doesn't contain a description of all of them, just explains the common items that I typically see relevant, based on my experiences with multiple customers.

  • Classical MRP list: This is one of the main items, and in most cases applies to those customers who are still running the classic MRP as part of their process. The majority decided to keep it due to the restriction by using MRP live and/or some custom developments where MRP list tables need to be read. In S/4HANA the MRP live code has an optimized logic, where is no longer needed the creation of an MRP list without causing a huge concern from a performance point of view. In addition, the evaluation based on the MRP list isn't optimal because is not going to consider new requirements that could be created meanwhile the finished of the MRP run and the real-time when the planners have to do the checking, because is just and snapshot of the result.

In general, the creation of the MRP list by running the classic MRP won't be possible anymore after the end of 2025, but the classic MPR will still work for those customers that have other restrictions to be considered before moving to MRP live.

Now what happens if we still need the MRP list creation? The best suggestion will be to check always read stock/requirements lists via API MD_MDPSX_READ_API or BAPI BAPI_MATERIAL_STOCK_REQ_LIST., as suggested in the SAP Note 2268085. In addition, SAP offers the standard report RMDMRPEXTRACT01, which extracts the planning data and saves it into a custom table, similar to the results generated by the MRP list. You can find more information about the MRP lust alternative by checking the blog MRP Lists are gone with MRP Live! What should I do now?

  • Flow Manufacturing: Flow manufacturing in ERP includes functions for line design, line balancing as well and rate/takt-based sequence planning. It is part of repetitive manufacturing solution and only suitable for repetitive manufacturing planned orders. Because is now part of the compatibility scope, means that you need to migrate from ERP line design, ERP line balancing, and ERP sequence planning to its designated alternative functionality which is now fully covered by PPDS model mix planning and sequencing.
  • Graphical Planning Table: Currently there are a bunch of troubles and open incidents due to the complexity when managing the chart and graphical planning tables provided in SAP GUI. Even when the transactions CM21, CM22, CM23, CM25, with graphical planning table or Gantt Chart are still available in SAP S/4HANA on-premise are not part of the target architecture in S/4HANA and remain as obsolete. To have a better user experience and make easier the understanding and the analysis by the planners, the recommendation is to replace those by using new Fiori Apps like "Manage Work Center Capacity", "Capacity Scheduling Table", and "Capacity Scheduling Board" in SAP S/4HANA. If you are interested in knowing more about this new application on PP-CP, please check my previous blog: Did you know this about Capacity Planning in S/4HANA?

In addition, you can consider SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud - DMC component Resource Orchestration REO. REO works on more granular data defined in DMC. In a job-shop environment, for example, work-centers could be defined in SAP S/4HANA and the individual machines only in DMC.

Last but not least, is important to know you can also have the possible to use embedded PP/DS if exists complex scheduling problems, for example, planned and production orders of different BOM levels need to be synchronized, or if material availability needs to be considered in scheduling, or if multiple bottleneck resources must be aligned by optimizing the schedule.

  • Logistics Information System In PP:  LIS data can be analyzed based on statistical data, which is contained in "information structures". Important key figures are directly updated from the operative application to the information structures. Where historically, Historically, following LIS tables (mentioned below) were used for standard or custom reports in Production Planning (S021, SFIS,  S022, SFIS, S023, SFIS, S024, and S026). However, LIS in PP is part of the S/4HANA compatibility pack and even when you can continue using LIS tables, and are available, won’t be considered as part of the new technologies anymore.

Considering future production analytics will be based on HANA, CDS views will be able to aggregate transactional data dynamically, and powerful analytical UIs for multi-dimensional reporting, resulting in the best option to replace the current LIS. Once switching you will note improvements in the performance of many transactions (fewer tables to update, fewer locking conflicts) and less memory consumption. Please refer to the following blog for further information: Custom Analytical Queries as an alternative for LIS in SAP S/4HANA Production Planning (PP)

  • Forecast-Based Planning: Forecast-based planning (MRP type VV) is not strategic anymore within SAP S/4HANA and should be avoided if possible. This is because the MRP creates planned orders or purchase requisitions if the total forecast demand up to a certain point of time exceeds the total firmed receipt quantity up to the same point of time. The total firmed receipt quantity includes inventory, production orders, purchase orders, firmed planned orders, and firmed purchase requisitions.

An alternative exists and is by using PIRs (Planned Independent Requirement) instead of forecast demands. Provides several advantages in S/4, allowing benefits like APIs available to handle PIRs, new FIORI-apps like 'Maintain PIRs' for better management, PIRs allow for consumption with sales demands and dependent requirements or stock transfer demands, the possibility to split PIRS and re-organize unused PIR demands.

So what should we do if MRP type VV is still in place? First, convert forecast requirements into planned independent requirements (PIR) and then change MRP type VV to one of the MRP types as PD, P1, P2, P3, or P4 which doesn’t have direct consideration of forecast.

  • Control Recipes/Instructions: Due to the several improvements in the used communication technology (currently via t-RFC), the usage of control recipes/control instructions in conjunction with browser-based PI sheets and work instructions is part of the SAP S/4HANA compatibility scope. As an alternative, it is possible to keep using the control recipes/control instructions in conjunction with linking process control systems and other external systems by using Manufacturing Execution Connect (classic).

SAP S/4HANA provides new Process Order integration capabilities with new SOAP and oData APIs, where the Control Instruction capabilities will be offered now as part of SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud and can be sent to MES, e.g. to SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud, with new SOAP APIs. Please refer to  SAP S/4HANA Feature Scope Description or note 3035649 FAQ : Manufacturing Execution Connect (classic).

A similar recommendation applies for Process Messages, also an item relevant for PP in compatibility scope, you can check further details on the SAP Note 2270231

  • Process Instruction Sheets: Browser-based PI sheets are used in the regulated environment (pharmaceutical production, and so on) to control and track the production execution. The content of the PI Sheet/Work Instruction is rendered as plain HTML and displayed in the SAP HTML Viewer of ABAP SAP GUI screens and depends on MS Internet Explorer. The new architecture recommends the usage of Digital Manufacturing Cloud’s (DMC) Production Operator Dashboard (POD) which provides similar functionality as PI-sheets. It will allow displaying Work Instructions, recording of actual data, and enforcing predefined production processes.

Please be careful and after switching from browser-based PI Sheets/Electronic Work Instructions to SAP DMC, remember to check your custom code in the context of data transfer from browser-based PI Sheets/Electronic Work Instructions to whatever receiver and clean up your custom code that makes use of this functionality.

Hope you find helpful the information provided in terms of understanding the compatibility pack and start to think about the action plan to complete the replacement of the function and adopt the S/4HANA strategy within the given timeline.

Further information:

SAP Support Portal

The Future of Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA

Do not forget to follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA, or myself via @Jennicherrera and LinkedIn: Jennifer Carolina Herrera Suárez | LinkedIn

Have a nice day!

Regards

Jennifer

entry.title: Re: Developing & Deploying the UI5 App to Cloud Foundry and Accessing from App-router End-to-End
entry.content:
Hello

Excellent contribution in this blog, I was following step by step and had no problems, everything was executed as is. However, when trying to connect to an OData On-Premise service, it generates an Internal Server Error.

Could you guide me on this error? I share the Destination with you

entry.title: Re: Can´t confirm a warehouse task via RF - Warehouse ord...
entry.content: Hi Marius. Thanks for your replies. A colleague just figured out the missing part. It was on the CBC: Define HY types for each warehouse number and assign HU. The HU type group I´ve created following the settingup instructions wasn't assigned to the Handling Unit Types per Warehouse Number. After that the confirmation of queues via RF is ok. Best Regards Victor Villarpando

entry.title: Re: how to display error message for choosing incorrect C...
entry.content:
Hi Chandra,

The right approach should be to control the CR types which are available in popup using Security roles.

There should be only certain set of users who should be able to access a certain CR type.

In your use case what is the criteria for the incorrect CR type?

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Please wait while the document is being processed
entry.content:
I have the same problem as described in this post:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/please-wait-while-the-document-being-processed/qaq-p/10592689

The solution appears to be in a KB article sited in the post (where it says "See this KBA"), but the link is broken.  I have had no luck with searching the knowledge base.  Can someone please re-post the link to the sited KB article?

Thanks

entry.title: Narrowing the Digital Divide with AI Accessibility for the Global South
entry.content:
The Western world is quickly adjusting to the increased use of AI technology and support, though some people remain apprehensive about its benefits and risks. To demystify AI tech, corporations are recognizing the value of ensuring AI literacy in the workforce.

This strategy aims to reduce employees' fears and apprehensions and foster an upskilled, more adaptable team. An increased familiarity with AI is expected to help employees understand the benefits of using AI, such as making tasks easier and assisting employees to achieve their professional goals. 

This is also an opportunity for educational organizations to make AI tools more accessible to marginalized groups, especially in countries of the Global South. A further narrowing of the “Digital Divide” is possible by establishing modern technology inclusivity at this early stage of AI. The potential for rural innovation through the use of AI technology paints an exciting picture of the future – one where collective input becomes a global reality.

Barefoot College International (BCI), a grassroots women-centric educational and vocational non-profit organization, sees the potential of including AI in its rurally focused initiatives. It offers its programs to rural and Indigenous women regardless of their prior level of formal education. This in turn unleashes women’s innate wisdom and aspirations – they’ve witnessed the capacity for learning despite a previous lack of learning resources. 

By showing real-world examples of how AI can benefit them, marginalized communities can bypass any apprehensions toward this new technology. Introducing AI simply as an extension of other digital tools reveals an opportunity for remote communities to embrace modern technology at an accelerated rate. 

Addressing 15 of the 17 UN Sustainability Development Goals (SDGs) through its programs, BCI works tirelessly to tailor its learning programs to current needs and challenges. Three of the 15 UNSDGs they address have shown successful headway in creating communities that are more sustainable and resilient to the risks of Climate Change. 

SDG #3: Good Health and Wellbeing

AI can function as a remote monitoring and support tool. In areas where medical facilities and workers are limited, AI support can provide assistance and reassurance to mothers and families about their health. It can also function as an educational tool that is adept at gathering reputable information efficiently for those who are less technologically savvy. Women's and girl’s mental health can also be addressed through AI assistance, especially for those without local access to such resources.

SDG #7: Affordable and Clean Energy

The use of AI to help with energy accessibility and distribution could be a game-changer for rurally based communities. Factors such as natural disaster predictability, supply chain sourcing and energy demand forecasting can aid communities in investing in the right kinds of renewable energy for their region. AI is also being used to help decentralized energy, such as solar, be distributed most cost-effectively through smart grids.

SDG #8: Decent Work and Economic Growth

Local demand for products and goods varies greatly. AI tools could assist rural female entrepreneurs by helping them discern which products people in their region are most likely to buy, or predict up-and-coming purchasing trends. Support including material sourcing, resource optimization and sustainable supply chain connections are examples of the many ways AI can help small rural business owners thrive.

To learn more about BCI, or to support their ongoing work focused on women, digital education, and sustainability, please visit their website www.barefootcollege.org.

The SAP SuccessFactors HR research team has released its latest article about AI Literacy in the workplace. You can also learn more about SAP’s “zero emissions, zero waste, and zero inequality” approach to sustainability.

entry.title: Re: A few updates from SAP Business One Support
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Indeed, the links are tricky → a new KBA has been released, the link in the KBA should work for partners: 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3525031

Enjoy!

Jesper

entry.title: Re: PR Workflow - automatic approval of 3.rd step when th...
entry.content: Hi Marius, do you have any update for us regarding the problem with the BADl MMPUR_WFL_CONTEXT_ENHANCE as described below?

entry.title: Dropdown bug (API level 34 I think) and slow performance
entry.content:
Hi all.
Some days ago, I build my app to reach the api level 24, required by google.
In the sap emulator, everything works fine, but when published, the dropdown components crash.
To solve I had to remove everyone and implement a custom dropdown, in a rush!.

Now, some users are experimenting a really slow performance, detected at this moment in Samsung Galaxy A02, Nokia 2.3 and Samsung A5. In the same devices, the older app worked fine.

I am struggling to find out how can the performance be improved, and cannot find a way.
Do you have similar problems?
Any clue to improve performance? I am using Recycler View when drawing repeated items, etc.

Or, is there a way to debug what is delaying my app? (the api's works really good)

Thanks

entry.title: How do I read an FTP server file from a BTP ABAP environment?
entry.content: I have one client requirement , to read file from FTP Server in BTP ABAP environment.

entry.title: Re: Have SAP HANA Database Explorer Extension for VSCode...
entry.content: Thanks for the feedback. I have passed this on to our development team for further consideration and will report back if there updates on this.

entry.title: Re: Ariba: In SCC-SBN how to send email notification to t...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Five emails can be included in the cXML, while the Notifications preference in the buyer admin portal allows you to set up to three alternative emails in case a PO does not include purchasing agent information. 

In the cXML of your PO document, the emails can be added inside the <OrderRequestHeader>...</OrderRequestHeader> tags, as shown on page 112 in this cxml reference guide. Here's an example: 

<Contact role="purchasingAgent">
...
   <Email>*EMAIL*</Email>
.....
</Contact>

entry.title: Re: Fiori with bdc togetherness
entry.content: Could you solve it in the meantime? Especially the part, where the users get all the BDC Popups?

entry.title: Re: Have SAP HANA Database Explorer Extension for VSCode...
entry.content: Thank you so much

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content: R&D confirmed both x86 and x64 exporting to ODBC has an issue so that's what they will be fixing. Go here for the update when it's available: https://origin.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/site/index.html

entry.title: Re: Interchangeability groups in MRP Area
entry.content:
Hi,  so far i got now reply.  I checked the S/4 System and i found coding from SAP which make it system wise impossible to create Interchangeability groups for MRP Area without modification.

Coding you can find in the

Programm /SAPAPO/SAPLRRP_PACK_MAINTAIN

 Form UPDATE_MARC_CHANGES_IN_DB Line 340 until 351

entry.title: Re: How to redeem a campaign code to get a free SAP Certification exam attempt and schedule your exa
entry.content:
Hello David,

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or undeserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I am willing to learn more about SAP and to undergo the rigors of studying for SAP Certification. I have completed my first learning journey Project Manage SAP S/4 Hana Implementation and I awaiting my certification attempt

Thank you

Ahmed

entry.title: Re: How to Use Custom Fields for Pricing in Sales Documents
entry.content:
Hi @Mertcan,
Currently there is no option to update prices automatically. What is your exact requirement? In which cases to you want to update prices automatically?

There is already a similar feature request in SAP Customer Influence where you can vote for to give it more priority in planning.
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/292742

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Wien 07.11.2024

entry.title: Re: how to download application log message through custom program?
entry.content: Application Log – Guidelines for Developers (BC-SRV-BAL)

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Wien 07.11.2024
entry.content: BTW: ich werde auch später kommen. haben jour fixe und ich nutze die Gelegenheit für #sitVIE-promotion.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Olga 

Our S4 system has been copied into a sandbox and the sum process has been run, and the Deletion transports where not included or generated. (we are using the CCM on BTP)

Can we still generate these maybe somewhere else and the import them after the SUM process ??

The proper should have been. (broadly only regardign to CCM and SUM)

1. Create a copy of the ECC system to upgrade in the Sandbox

2. Run the analysis of CCM and scoping on the sandbox based on the ASUG data,

3  generate the Deletion transport

4, Run the SUM process cand include the transport in the SUM  process to convert the system.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Applications Studio 2411 (Pilot) can not do...
entry.content: Hi Anant, we are facing the same issue. could we ask what do you mean by backend chagnes and how we can correct those? Thanks!

entry.title: Unable to create text with text rich editor
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

The option of text rich edition for text fields is disable can anyone tell me where is the option to enable it?

entry.title: Re: BFC: Trigger condition in formula based on RU?
entry.content:
Hello, can I have two trigger conditions? Here is an example of the formula and the two trigger conditions I would like to add:

Formula: {AC in (FAMREF=BL1-F0002) ; FL in (F20; F30; F40; F50; F60; F61; F62; F70)} = {DP = MONTH(-1; REF_PER) ; PE = MONTH(-1; REF_PER)}

Trigger conditions: [DP > DFIRST] AND [RU in (ETYPE=S)]

For your information, I tried to include the second condition ([RU in (ETYPE=S)]) in the formula, but it didn't work.

Thank you for helping me solve the issue

entry.title: Re: How do I read an FTP server file from a BTP ABAP envi...
entry.content:
I hope you're talking about sFTP.

I would think you have to use SAP Cloud Integration to achieve this.

entry.title: Maintenance order printing getting time out
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are printing the maintenance order from edit mode and from additional functions to print.

If the number of operations in the maintenance order is more (say 75 in our case) if we use the print queue and sent an output, it is throwing an error "500 internal server error".

So, instead of using the print queue we click on print preview so that the pdf will display and then printing it manually, but while doing it loading of PDF is taking the more time.

Could you please let us know the resolution/reason for the inconsistency. 

Regards,

Yaswanth

 @Ravisha 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content: Wow, that's insane that it took more than 4 years for this to be addressed. Well, now I can honestly say I was involved in making Crystal Reports better....for what it's worth. 😄

entry.title: Re: How to configured XBRL options in SAP DM?
entry.content: Hi Marc, I would also need some documentation. Can I send you an email?

entry.title: Re: Consumption of an API within a Custom POD Plugin in S...
entry.content: In the plugin controller you can create an URL like this example for the get work center public API: var url = this.getPublicApiRestDataSourceUri() + "workcenter/v1/workcenters?plant=" + plant + "&workCenter=" + workCenter; Then you can use this.ajaxGetRequest wo make the API call for this URL

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content:
What version of MS SQL Server are you using?

According to the CR Platforms info ODBC 19 is only supported in MS SQL 2022:

 

2019ODBC BI 4.3 SP01+Microsoft ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server
OLEDB BI 4.3 SP01+Microsoft OLEDB Driver 18 for SQL Server
JDBCBI 4.3 SP01+Microsoft JDBC Driver 8.2 for SQL Server
ODBCBI 4.3 SP01+DataDirect ODBC (10)
2022ODBC BI 4.3 SP04+Microsoft ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server
OLEDB BI 4.3 SP04+Microsoft OLEDB Driver 19 for SQL Server
JDBCBI 4.3 SP04+Microsoft JDBC Driver 12.2 for SQL Server
ODBCBI 4.3 SP04+DataDirect ODBC (10)

So what CR does is when the DB Client dll cannot be found what it does is default to a generic SQL Syntax, likely cause why the Syntax for the Parameter is different.

The other option is test using an ODBC connection, just as a test, to see if ODBC generates the correct syntax.

Note the patch level is the same for CR 2020 Designer as noted for BI 4.3

entry.title: Re: How do I read an FTP server file from a BTP ABAP envi...
entry.content: Could you please share some information on FTP Cloud integration for blogs, specifically for reading files? It could be really helpful for me.

Regards

entry.title: Re: ERR! CRWEB00000119
entry.content:
Likely cause is you don't have the database client installed or configured to work on the CRS Server.

Or you don't have permissions to access the DB, CR fully supports Database Security.

Check with your CRS Administrator to see if they get the same error and if you have permissions to view that report.

entry.title: Re: Upload Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable...
entry.content: Hi team, the problem is that import supplier invoices or upload customer open items apps are only to upload invoices or credit memo. It is not possible to post other type of posting. Is there another solution?

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content: Actually 4 years is misleading. Without doing a bunch of testing it's possible this was only an issue since some 2016 patch may have broke it....

entry.title: Re: Forbidden AI Launchpad
entry.content: Same here...

entry.title: Re: TSL field value in ACDOCU is not the same as in ACDOCA
entry.content:
change the breakdown category per note 3144691  and/or import the notes 3204008 , 3302620 

note 3144691 - Group Reporting: Data release of transaction currency amount
note 3204008 - Data Release Task: Inaccurate transaction currency and transaction currency amount
note 3302620 - RUJ: Evaluate Breakdown Category for Transaction Currency

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA PPDS con IBP SUPPLY, REQUERIMIENTOS HEURISTIC SAP_PP_002
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working whit  SAP PPDS that is embedded with SAP S/4 HANA version 2023.

We run the heuristic SAP_PP_002 to generate the planned orders.

This heuristic considers the following requirements:

1) Sales forecast

2) Sales orders

3) Safety stock

4) Stock transport requests

The SAP S/4 HANA PPDS is coexisting with the IBP Supply, and this generates the KEY FIGURE TARGET.

In some runs we are going to take the above 4 requirements, but in other runs we will only take the KEY FIGURE TARGET.

I have the following questions:

1) How can I get the heuristic SAP_PP_002 to not consider the 4 requirements above and only consider the KEY FIGURE TARGET?

2) Or what other heuristic can I use to take only the KET FIGURE TARGET as a requirement?

3) Is there any BADI, BAPI or ENHANCEMENT that can help me?

Best regards,

Arnulfo Cordero Pradel

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs
entry.content:
Hello David,
I identify as a person in the below points :
-people in remote area not have the same
-minorities who may not have equal opportunities in their geographic locations
-first generation college student
-people looking to switch their careers mid life

Regards

entry.title: How to trigger a workflow when doing a Temporary Change on a Work Schedule?
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I found this ( Workflows for Temporary Changes | SAP Help Portal )information by SAP that explains how to trigger an approval workflow when creating a Temporary Change on the employee's Work Schedule through the Administer Time menu. I followed the information and testes several scenarios but did not manage to trigger the workflow. 

 

Did you ever did a configuration like this before? What could possibly be the error that I am doing?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Is it possible to change sales orders through spreadsheets?
entry.content:
Hello,

we have the requirement to mass change sales orders and add new schedule line items to existing sales orders through spreadsheets. Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change sales orders through spreads...
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's not possible to change existing sales orders through spreadsheets, you can only create new ones.
Please check the official documentation here: Import Sales Orders | SAP Help Portal

However, you can accomplish your requirement through Sales APIs. It's possible to mass change sales orders and add new items to existing sales orders through the following APIs:

Sales Order (A2X, OData V2) | SAP Help Portal
Operations for Sales Order (A2X, OData V2) | SAP Help Portal

Sales Order (A2X, OData V4) | SAP Help Portal
Operations for Sales Order (A2X, OData V4) | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @S0022323026 ,

if you haven't created the deletion transport on the ECC system before conversion you can create a  new custom code analysis project in the CCM ap on SAP BTP to analyze the code in your converted S4 sandbox, upload the collected usage data and create deletion transport on the S/4 sandbox. We recommend to backup then the unused code, see also the blog How to backup custom code with gCTS.

Kind regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Elimination postings in group reporting
entry.content: My understanding is that you want to see all eliminations on PL 20 and PL30, then you need to specify Group value in the prompt and only bring the consolidation unit dimension rather then consolidation unit eliminated dimension as well as posting level into the layout?

entry.title: Re: Keep the original text in balance carry forward items instead on "BCF" in Group Report
entry.content: TEXT in ACDOCU is derived from the name of each GR automation task.

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework featured by LeanIX EAM
entry.content:
Good work.

@Rene_de_Daniel , A couple of questions,

What is the future of SAP Enterprise Architecture Designer ?

Is there any path to migrate from EAD to LeanIX?

Regards,

Brian

entry.title: Join Us in Shaping the Future with the SAP Business Technology Platform!
entry.content:
At SAP, we believe that collaboration is key to driving innovation. We’re excited to invite our valued customers and partners to engage in a series of groundbreaking projects leveraging the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This powerful platform integrates data management, analytics, application development, and intelligent technologies, enabling you to unlock new possibilities for your business.

Why Get Involved?

Participating in these projects allows you to harness the full potential of the SAP BTP. With its low-code development capabilities, cloud security features, and AI-driven solutions, the platform empowers you to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. Your insights and expertise are crucial in shaping tools that align with your unique needs and challenges.

Explore Our Upcoming Projects

We’re focusing on a variety of innovative initiatives built on the capabilities of SAP BTP, including:

  • Low-Code Development: Simplify application creation and process automation to enhance agility.
  • Cloud Security Enhancements: Strengthen your security posture with advanced encryption and key management solutions.
  • AI-Powered Insights: Leverage AI to transform unstructured data into actionable insights.
  • Automation and Integration: Discover how to automate processes seamlessly across your organization.

How to Participate

If you’re interested in getting involved, we’d love to hear from you! Reach out to us to express your interest in participating in these exciting initiatives. Together, we can leverage the SAP Business Technology Platform to drive innovation and create impactful solutions that help your organization thrive.

Thank you for being part of our journey. We look forward to collaborating with you to shape the future of technology!

Register until November 15, 2024 and take advantage of this opportunity!

SAP Business Technology Platform projects for registration:

 

  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Investigation into a Customer Security Dashboard
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • Cloud Security: Improving the Encryption Key Management Experience
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data
  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

If you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration, see here the full list.

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: SAP Kernal release 793 for Personas 3.0 SP16 shows in...
entry.content: Thanks Tamas!

entry.title: Re: Email no arquivo de remessa itau
entry.content:
Olá pessoal, estou com essa mesma questão, alguém consegue ajudar por favor?

Como é gerada a linha de REGISTRO 5 que contém as informações do email? Onde customizamos para que o arquivo leve o email do cliente?

#ITAU #I109 #CNAB400

Obrigada

entry.title: Company code numbering release 2408
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I would like to know if, in release 2408, the logic for numbering the company code has changed.

In CBC, when I define the organisation structure and thus also the company code, is it possible to use a letter as the initial character in the four digits, or must I use a number as the first character? E.g.: "IT10".

Thank you

Francesco
